behavior: Aerial insectivore; catches flying insects in agile, low, swooping flight with quick turns and little gliding.
field_marks: Slender, streamlined swallow with very long, narrow, pointed wings.
Adult tail is long and deeply forked, with elongated outer streamers extending beyond the wingtips.
Steely blue to blue-black crown, back, wings, and tail.
Cinnamon to chestnut forehead and throat contrast with the dark blue face.
Underparts range from tawny or buff to pale; small white spots beneath the tail may show in flight.
Short, dark, broad-based bill and small dark legs.
habitat: Open and semi-open country, including fields, grasslands, marshes, ponds, and coastal water; commonly forages over open ground or water and nests on sheltered human-made structures such as eaves, buildings, and bridges.

sources.title: Barn Swallow Identification — All About Birds, Cornell Lab of Ornithology
